months.name: January
months.summary: Deep winter: peak aurora, dog sledding, empty trails. Brutal Interior cold, few tourists.

months.name: February
months.summary: Aurora peak continues with longer light. Fur Rondy and ice-art season begin. Cold, clear, cheap.

months.name: March
months.summary: Best mix of aurora and daylight. Iditarod, ski touring, spring-break energy. Still very cold.

months.name: April
months.summary: Shoulder season: aurora fades by month-end, snow melts to mud. Arctic Man, quiet, low prices.

months.name: May
months.summary: Summer switch flips: roads and railroad open, cruises start, whales and puffins return. Green-up.

months.name: June
months.summary: Long days, wildflowers, king salmon run. Denali buses roll, midnight sun peaks. Busy and pricey.

months.name: July
months.summary: Peak season: warmest, Brooks Falls bears, sockeye run, festivals. Most crowded, most expensive.

months.name: August
months.summary: Silvers run, fireweed tops out, aurora returns late month. Fall colors start. Busy, rain rises.

months.name: September
months.summary: Fall colors peak, aurora resumes, termination dust dusts peaks. Fewer crowds, cruises wind down.

months.name: October
months.summary: Freeze-up shoulder: summer gone, winter not yet set. Strong aurora, low prices, most tours closed.

months.name: November
months.summary: Early winter, deep dark, strong aurora. Snow builds, dog-sled season starts. Very quiet and cheap.

months.name: December
months.summary: Darkest month with holiday lights and aurora. Cold, snowy, quiet outside solstice festivities.

windows.label: Denali Park bus season
windows.note: Buses to ~Mile 43 (Polychrome); road beyond closed by Pretty Rocks slide until ~2027.

windows.label: Cruise season
windows.note: Inside Passage & Gulf sailings; peak Jun-Aug for weather and wildlife.

windows.label: Alaska Railroad summer service
windows.note: Daily Denali Star, Coastal Classic, Glacier Discovery on full schedule.

windows.label: Alaska Railroad winter (Aurora Winter Train)
windows.note: Weekends only, Anchorage-Fairbanks; slower flag-stop service.

windows.label: Brooks Falls bears - summer peak
windows.note: Sockeye leap the falls; most bears and most people on the platform.

windows.label: Brooks Falls bears - fall peak
windows.note: Fewer crowds; fat bears feast on spawned-out salmon before denning.

windows.label: King (Chinook) salmon run
windows.note: Peak mid-June; Kenai, Kasilof, Nushagak, Copper R. Kenai kings often restricted.

windows.label: Red (sockeye) salmon run
windows.note: Copper R mid-May; Russian R June; Kenai late run peaks mid-late July.

windows.label: Pink (humpy) salmon run
windows.note: Strong even-year returns across Southcentral; easy shore fishing.

windows.label: Chum (dog) salmon run
windows.note: Common in Interior and Arctic rivers; overlaps pink and sockeye runs.

windows.label: Silver (coho) salmon run
windows.note: Peak late Aug-Sep; last salmon of the season, prized by anglers.

windows.label: Halibut season
windows.note: Charters from Homer, Seward, Valdez; prime May-Sep, weather-dependent.

windows.label: Aurora season
windows.note: Dark-sky window; best on clear cold nights near the equinoxes (Sep, Mar).

windows.label: Midnight sun
windows.note: Utqiagvik sun never sets ~May 11-Aug 2; Fairbanks ~22h light at solstice.

windows.label: Fall colors
windows.note: Interior and Denali tundra turns first; birch and aspen gold by early Sep.

windows.label: Termination dust
windows.note: First snow dusting on mountain peaks; local sign that summer is over.

windows.label: Iditarod Sled Dog Race
windows.note: Ceremonial start Anchorage, restart Willow; 2027 race starts Mar 6.

windows.label: Fur Rondy (Fur Rendezvous)
windows.note: Anchorage winter festival; sled races and Running of the Reindeer.

windows.label: World Ice Art Championships
windows.note: Fairbanks; multi-block ice carving, park open evenings into late March.

windows.label: Arctic Man Ski & Sno-Go Classic
windows.note: Summit Lake near Paxson; ski-snowmachine race and huge backcountry camp.

windows.label: Humpback whale season
windows.note: Arrive Southeast/Kenai Fjords/PWS in May; depart to Hawaii Oct-Nov.

windows.label: Gray whale spring migration
windows.note: Pass Kodiak and Kenai coast northbound; peak viewing in April.

windows.label: Puffin season
windows.note: Horned and tufted puffins nest at Gull Island, Kenai Fjords, St. Paul.

windows.label: Wildflowers & fireweed
windows.note: Alpine blooms Jun-Jul; fireweed tops out late Aug, ~6 weeks to snow.

windows.label: Mosquito season
windows.note: Peak mid-Jun to mid-Jul, worst in Interior wetlands; bring DEET/headnet.

windows.label: Denali Highway (gravel)
windows.note: 135 mi Paxson-Cantwell; unmaintained in winter, gates close with snow.

windows.label: Hatcher Pass summit road
windows.note: Willow-side gate above Independence Mine opens only after snow clears.

windows.label: McCarthy Road
windows.note: 60 mi gravel Chitina-McCarthy; slow, no winter maintenance.

windows.label: Nabesna Road
windows.note: 42 mi into Wrangell-St. Elias; some stream fords, best mid-summer.

windows.label: Dalton Highway
windows.note: Open all year to Deadhorse; best Jun-Aug, winter = ice and whiteouts.
